userStories.title: Enable SSR Foundation
userStories.description: Upgrade the Astro blog to an SSR-capable foundation and add the minimal configuration surface needed for Stripe-backed subscription flows.
acceptance_criteria: Astro is switched from pure static output to a Node SSR-capable setup so server API routes can be built and run.
A shared config layer exists for Stripe-related environment variables, with secret values only read from server-side code paths.
The app can still build when Stripe variables are missing, exposing a non-broken fallback state instead of crashing.
suggested_tests: test -f src/env.d.ts || test -f src/lib/stripe/config.ts || test -f src/config/stripe.ts

userStories.title: Add Checkout Session API
userStories.description: Implement the real server-side Stripe Checkout session creation logic as the core payment entry point.
acceptance_criteria: A server endpoint exists to create Stripe Checkout Sessions for subscriptions.
The endpoint uses Stripe price IDs for monthly and yearly plans and sets `mode: subscription`.
The endpoint reads `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` only on the server and does not introduce any email-lookup-based subscription management flow.
Success and cancel URLs are derived from `SITE_URL` or an equivalent server-safe base URL strategy.

userStories.title: Build Homepage Subscription Section
userStories.description: Add the public subscription area on the homepage, including monthly/yearly purchase actions, a secure billing-management entry, and a graceful unconfigured state.
acceptance_criteria: The homepage includes a visible subscription section that matches the site's existing editorial or magazine design language instead of a SaaS dashboard pattern.
The page contains stable markers `data-subscribe-tier="monthly"`, `data-subscribe-tier="yearly"`, and `data-billing-portal-link`.
The UI clearly explains that the subscription supports ongoing writing and updates, and distinguishes monthly from yearly plans.
When Stripe env vars are not configured, the section shows a graceful fallback message without broken actions or an ugly placeholder.
The subscription management entry uses the hosted Stripe customer portal login link from configuration, or an equally safe hosted flow, without asking users to enter an email for lookup.

userStories.title: Document Env and Smoke Verification
userStories.description: Document the local configuration and testing path so another developer can run and verify the subscription skeleton without guessing.
acceptance_criteria: README or a dedicated local setup doc explains how to configure `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`, `STRIPE_PRICE_MONTHLY`, `STRIPE_PRICE_YEARLY`, `STRIPE_CUSTOMER_PORTAL_URL`, and `SITE_URL`.
The documentation explains the local test path for the homepage subscription section and the checkout endpoint, including what fallback behavior to expect without real Stripe secrets.
The documented verification path includes at least the default build check and a smoke check for the required homepage markers and secure management-link approach.
